info.title: Local API unreachable during login attempt

parts.text:
Welcome to AudioReach
Enter your details to access your account

Email
jonnadaraju147@gmail.com
Password
•••••••••••
Cannot reach API. Tried: http://localhost:8000 (Failed to fetch); http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Failed to fetch)

The user is seeing a login page for "AudioReach" that can't connect to the API at localhost:8000. This appears to be a frontend application that's trying to connect to a backend API server that's not running.

Let me explore the codebase to understand what's happening and how to start the backend server.
Let me check the README and package.json files to understand how to start the backend server.

state.output:
<path>/mnt/c/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Audio Recorder & Playback System with AI Agents + MCP
2: 
3: End-to-end audio platform with:
4: 
5: - FastAPI backend (JWT auth, upload, metadata, streaming)
6: - PostgreSQL + async SQLAlchemy
7: - React frontend (recording, playback, search, AI assistant UI)
8: - MCP server exposing recording intelligence tools
9: - AI agent orchestration for multi-step reasoning over recordings
10: - CLI acceptance tests + AI CLI commands
11: 
12: ## Table of Contents
13: 
14: 1. System Overview
15: 2. Architecture
16: 3. Project Structure
17: 4. Core Features
18: 5. AI Features
19: 6. MCP Server
20: 7. Agent Orchestration
21: 8. API Reference
22: 9. Database & Migrations
23: 10. Setup Guide
24: 11. CLI Usage
25: 12. Security & Safety
26: 13. Troubleshooting
27: 14. Next Improvements
28: 
29: ## 1) System Overview
30: 
31: The system allows authenticated users to:
32: 
33: - Record/upload audio
34: - Store audio files and metadata
35: - List, play, and delete recordings
36: - Transcribe recordings
37: - Search recordings semantically (vector search)
38: - Summarize recordings
39: - Ask questions across recording transcripts
40: 
41: ## 2) Architecture
42: 
43: ### Backend
44: 
45: - Framework: FastAPI
46: - Auth: JWT bearer tokens
47: - ORM: SQLAlchemy async
48: - DB: PostgreSQL + pgvector
49: - AI service layer:
50:   - transcription
51:   - embeddings
52:   - summarization
53:   - question answering
54: 
55: ### Frontend
56: 
57: - React + TypeScript
58: - Audio capture via `MediaRecorder`
59: - Dashboard playback/list/search
60: - AI assistant chat panel with reasoning steps
61: 
62: ### AI/MCP
63: 
64: - MCP server (Python MCP SDK) provides structured tools
65: - Agent orchestrates tool calls based on user query intent
66: - Backend `/agent/query` also provides API-based orchestration with step traces
67: 
68: ## 3) Project Structure
69: 
70: ```text
71: agent/                         # MCP-connected agent orchestration
72:   __init__.py
73:   mcp_agent.py
74: backend/
75:   app/
76:     api/routes/                # auth, recordings, agent routes
77:       auth.py
78:       recordings.py
79:       agent.py
80:     core/                      # config, database, security
81:       config.py
82:       database.py
83:       security.py
84:     models/                    # SQLAlchemy models
85:       models.py
86:     schemas/                   # Pydantic schemas
87:       user.py
88:       recording.py
89:       agent.py
90:     services/                  # auth, recording, AI, agent services
91:       auth_service.py
92:       recording_service.py
93:       ai_service.py
94:       agent_service.py
95:       ai_guard.py
96:     main.py
97:   migrations/                  # SQL migration for AI extensions
98:   API_CONTRACT_AI.md           # AI endpoint contracts
99: cli/
100:   test_api.py                  # acceptance tests
101:   ai_cli.py                    # AI-focused CLI commands
102:   requirements.txt
103: frontend/
104:   src/
105:     pages/                     # UI pages
106:       Dashboard.tsx
107:       Dashboard.css
108:       Login.tsx
109:       Register.tsx
110:       Auth.css
111:     services/                  # frontend API client
112:       api.ts
113:     App.tsx
114:     App.css
115:     index.tsx
116:     useAudioRecorder.ts        # audio recording hook
117: mcp_server/
118:   server.py                    # MCP tool server
119:   requirements.txt
120:   README.md
121: ```
122: 
123: ## 4) Core Features
124: 
125: Already supported:
126: 
127: - User registration/login
128: - Audio upload
129: - File storage under `uploads/audio/<user_id>/...`
130: - Metadata persistence
131: - Recordings list
132: - Recording stream endpoint
133: - Delete recording
134: 
135: ## 5) AI Features
136: 
137: ### Data model extension
138: 
139: `AudioRecording` includes:
140: 
141: - `transcript` (`TEXT`)
142: - `transcript_embedding` (`VECTOR(1536)`)
143: 
144: ### AI endpoints
145: 
146: - `POST /recordings/{id}/transcribe`
147: - `POST /recordings/search`
148: - `POST /recordings/{id}/summarize`
149: - `POST /recordings/answer`
150: - `POST /agent/query`
151: 
152: Detailed payloads are documented in:
153: - [backend/API_CONTRACT_AI.md](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/backend/API_CONTRACT_AI.md)
154: 
155: ### Provider routing model
156: 
157: The backend supports split providers per capability:
158: 
159: - transcription provider
160: - embedding provider
161: - chat provider
162: 
163: Each can use separate `API_KEY`, `BASE_URL`, and `MODEL`, with fallback to `OPENAI_*`.
164: 
165: ## 6) MCP Server
166: 
167: MCP tool server:
168: - [mcp_server/server.py](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/mcp_server/server.py)
169: 
170: Tools:
171: 
172: - `list_recordings(user_id, token?)`
173: - `get_recording_metadata(recording_id, token?)`
174: - `transcribe_audio(recording_id, token?)`
175: - `summarize_audio(recording_id, token?)`
176: - `search_recordings(query, limit?, token?)`
177: - `answer_question_about_recordings(question, limit?, token?)`
178: 
179: All tool inputs/outputs are structured and JWT-scoped through backend APIs.
180: 
181: ## 7) Agent Orchestration
182: 
183: ### MCP-connected agent module
184: 
185: - [agent/mcp_agent.py](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/agent/mcp_agent.py)
186: 
187: Supported patterns:
188: 
189: - "Show my latest recording"
190: - "Summarize my latest recording"
191: - "Find recordings mentioning deadlines"
192: - "What did I say about the meeting?"
193: 
194: ### Backend API agent endpoint
195: 
196: - `POST /agent/query`
197: - Returns:
198:   - final `answer`
199:   - list of `steps` with tools + previews
200: 
201: ## 8) API Reference (quick)
202: 
203: ### Auth
204: 
205: - `POST /auth/register`
206: - `POST /auth/login`
207: 
208: ### Recordings
209: 
210: - `POST /recordings/upload`
211: - `GET /recordings`
212: - `GET /recordings/{id}`
213: - `DELETE /recordings/{id}`
214: - `GET /recordings/{id}/stream`
215: 
216: ### AI
217: 
218: - `POST /recordings/{id}/transcribe`
219: - `POST /recordings/search`
220: - `POST /recordings/{id}/summarize`
221: - `POST /recordings/answer`
222: - `POST /agent/query`
223: 
224: ## 9) Database & Migrations
225: 
226: Migration file:
227: 
228: - [backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql)
229: 
230: What it does:
231: 
232: - enables `vector` extension
233: - adds transcript fields
234: - adds ivfflat vector index
235: 
236: ## 10) Setup Guide
237: 
238: ### Prerequisites
239: 
240: - Python 3.12+
241: - Node.js 18+
242: - PostgreSQL with `pgvector`
243: 
244: ### Backend
245: 
246: ```powershell
247: cd backend
248: python -m venv venv
249: .\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
250: pip install -r requirements.txt
251: ```
252: 
253: Create `backend/.env` from `.env.example`.
254: 
255: Run migration:
256: 
257: ```sql
258: -- execute backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql
259: ```
260: 
261: Start API:
262: 
263: ```powershell
264: uvicorn app.main:app --reload
265: ```
266: 
267: ### Frontend
268: 
269: ```powershell
270: cd frontend
271: npm install
272: npm start
273: ```
274: 
275: ### MCP Server
276: 
277: ```powershell
278: cd mcp_server
279: python -m venv .venv
280: .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
281: pip install -r requirements.txt
282: python server.py
283: ```
284: 
285: ## 11) CLI Usage
286: 
287: ### Acceptance test suite
288: 
289: ```powershell
290: cd cli
291: python test_api.py run-tests
292: ```
293: 
294: ### AI commands
295: 
296: ```powershell
297: cd cli
298: python ai_cli.py transcribe-recording 42 --email you@example.com
299: python ai_cli.py summarize-recording 42 --email you@example.com
300: python ai_cli.py search-recordings "deadlines" --email you@example.com
301: python ai_cli.py ask-agent "What did I say about project deadlines?" --user-id 1 --email you@example.com
302: ```
303: 
304: ## 12) Security & Safety
305: 
306: Implemented controls:
307: 
308: - JWT enforcement for all user recording access
309: - user-scoped DB queries (`recording.user_id == current_user.id`)
310: - prompt-injection pattern blocking for AI query fields
311: - input sanitization + max length limits
312: - structured tool inputs/outputs in MCP
313: 
314: Additional recommended hardening:
315: 
316: - rate limiting per user for AI endpoints
317: - audit log for AI tool invocations
318: - strict MIME/codec checks on upload
319: - background jobs + retries for transcription
320: 
321: ## 13) Troubleshooting
322: 
323: ### `pgvector` not found
324: 
325: - Ensure PostgreSQL extension is installed and run:
326:   - `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;`
327: 
328: ### Transcription fails with OpenRouter
329: 
330: - Some providers may not support `/audio/transcriptions` the same way.
331: - Use split provider config:
332:   - chat via OpenRouter
333:   - transcription/embedding via OpenAI-compatible endpoint with those APIs.
334: 
335: ### `EMBEDDING_API_KEY (or OPENAI_API_KEY fallback) is not configured`
336: 
337: - Set at least one key in `backend/.env`:
338:   - `OPENAI_API_KEY=<your_key>` (shared fallback for all AI capabilities), or
339:   - `EMBEDDING_API_KEY=<your_key>` (embedding-only override)
340: - Restart the backend after editing `.env`.
341: 
342: ## 14) Next Improvements
343: 
344: 1. Replace raw SQL migrations with Alembic migration pipeline.
345: 2. Add async background worker for transcription/embedding jobs.
346: 3. Add integration tests for AI endpoints and agent flows.
347: 4. Add token usage/cost tracking for AI operations.

(End of file - total 347 lines)
</content>
state.title: README.md

metadata.preview:
# Audio Recorder & Playback System with AI Agents + MCP

End-to-end audio platform with:

- FastAPI backend (JWT auth, upload, metadata, streaming)
- PostgreSQL + async SQLAlchemy
- React frontend (recording, playback, search, AI assistant UI)
- MCP server exposing recording intelligence tools
- AI agent orchestration for multi-step reasoning over recordings
- CLI acceptance tests + AI CLI commands

## Table of Contents

1. System Overview
2. Architecture
3. Project Structure
4. Core Features
5. AI Features
6. MCP Server
7. Agent Orchestration

parts.text:
Now I understand the project structure. This is a full-stack audio recording application with:
- FastAPI backend
- React frontend
- PostgreSQL database with pgvector

The error shows the frontend is trying to connect to `http://localhost:8000` but the backend isn't running. Let me check if there's a backend folder and see if I can start the backend server.
The frontend is trying to connect to the backend API at `localhost:8000`, but the backend server isn't running. Let me check the backend setup and start it.
Now let me check if there's a virtual environment in the backend folder and try to start the backend server.
